skill.name: incident-command-governor
skill.description: Build governed incident command packets. Use when asked to standardize incident review, run severity assessment, or assemble incident communication.
skill.summary: Governed incident command packets with severity assessment, ownership tracking, and stakeholder communication.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 17 potential issues, all dismissed as false positives after review. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings at agents/interface.yaml:3 and SKILL.md:3 are plain text descriptions with no cryptographic code. The 15 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are standard Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `SEV1`, `references/severity-matrix.md`), not executable commands. The Python helper script performs simple JSON-to-Markdown transformation with no network access, eval, or shell execution. No malicious patterns were confirmed.

content.value_statement: Incident reviews often lack structure and audit trails. This skill builds governed incident command packets with explicit severity, ownership, and stakeholder communication blocks.
actual_capabilities: Gathers minimum incident inputs including summary, timeline, affected systems, and severity indicators
Assigns severity using the references/severity-matrix.md rules (SEV1, SEV2, SEV3)
Shapes communications by audience using references/comms-policy.md
Produces structured incident packets with summary, timeline, impact scope, owners, and governance notes
Assembles deterministic packets via scripts/build_incident_packet.py with JSON input
Surfaces missing evidence as explicit blockers rather than guessing
limitations: Does not execute or connect to live incident response systems
Requires structured JSON input for the deterministic packet builder
Does not replace human incident commander judgment or escalation decisions
Severity assignment depends on the quality of evidence provided

use_cases.title: Post-Incident Review Documentation
use_cases.description: Convert messy operational notes and transcripts into a structured, reviewable incident packet with clear severity and ownership.

use_cases.title: Governed Workflow Templates
use_cases.description: Build reusable incident handling workflows that follow monthly review cadence and explicit governance standards.

use_cases.title: Stakeholder Communication Drafting
use_cases.description: Generate audience-specific internal and external updates with proper scope control and simplification.
use_cases.target_user: Communications managers and customer success leads

prompt_templates.title: Basic Incident Packet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use incident-command-governor to build an incident packet from these notes: [paste timeline, affected systems, and owners].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for assembling a packet from existing notes

prompt_templates.title: Severity Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Read references/severity-matrix.md and assign severity to this incident: [describe event, customer impact, and data integrity risk].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need evidence-backed severity classification

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Split Communications
prompt_templates.prompt: Use incident-command-governor to draft internal responder updates and external stakeholder updates from this incident summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need separated communications for different audiences

prompt_templates.title: Deterministic Packet Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Run scripts/build_incident_packet.py with input/incident_input_example.json to generate a governed packet structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: For reproducible packet assembly with structured JSON input

output_examples.input: API latency spiked after a rollout, dashboard requests timed out for 14 minutes, rollback restored service.
output_examples.output: Incident packet with SEV1 severity (customer-facing outage), timeline of deploy through rollback, impact scope on dashboard API, owners for API on-call and release manager, and a governance note for next monthly review.

output_examples.input: Localized login failures for 5% of users in one region, no data loss, mitigated within 30 minutes.
output_examples.output: Incident packet with SEV3 severity (localized degradation), timeline with detection and mitigation, scoped impact note, and separation between internal responder communication and external customer update.

best_practices: Always cite evidence for severity assignments rather than guessing
Separate communications by audience and avoid reusing internal wording externally without simplification
Surface missing evidence as explicit blockers to maintain audit trail integrity
anti_patterns: Do not downgrade severity silently when evidence is incomplete; report uncertainty instead
Do not skip the review-policy check before finalizing a packet
Do not treat this skill as a replacement for human incident commander judgment

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a governed incident command packet with summary, severity, timeline, impact scope, owners, communications, and governance notes.

faq.question: Does it connect to external systems?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation and workflow skill that works from provided inputs and reference files.

faq.question: How is severity determined?
faq.answer: Severity is assigned using references/severity-matrix.md rules based on customer impact and data integrity risk.

faq.question: Can I use it for one-off updates?
faq.answer: It is designed for structured, repeatable incident workflows. For one-off explanations, use a general assistant instead.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in supported_tools.

faq.question: How is it maintained?
faq.answer: It follows a monthly review cadence under a governed lifecycle with explicit drift signals for policy or template changes.
